findings.issue: TDD Anchor test TestCollectorUpdateProcPidStat17Cols references 'NewTestConnect()' as the helper to open a real PG connection. This name does not match the existing helper pattern in the stat package. The stat package uses postgres.NewTestConnectVersion(version) from internal/postgres/testing.go. If the implementer uses a non-existent 'NewTestConnect()' name the test will fail to compile.
findings.fix: Replace 'NewTestConnect()' with 'postgres.NewTestConnectVersion(<version>)' (from internal/postgres/testing.go). Check existing stat_test.go or postgres_test.go for the exact call pattern already used in the package.

findings.issue: config_view.go does not currently import 'github.com/lesovsky/pgcenter/internal/stat'. Task 06 details section notes the import must be added. The statement 'The package is already imported in top/stat.go so no cycle is introduced' is technically correct (same top package, no cycle), but the import must still be added to config_view.go explicitly — Go requires per-file imports.
findings.fix: When implementing switchViewToProcPidStat, add 'github.com/lesovsky/pgcenter/internal/stat' to the import block in top/config_view.go. No circular import risk: internal/stat does not import top/.

findings.issue: dialog.go guard restructuring: the task's step 4 description of the 'first check' uses the phrase 'range (dialogFilter, dialogCancelQuery..dialogSetMask]' which is ambiguous. The intent is d > dialogFilter (=2) AND d < dialogChangeAge (=8), i.e., d in {3,4,5,6,7}. An implementer could misread this and use 'd >= dialogCancelQuery && d <= dialogSetMask' (explicit enum list) which is equivalent but fragile if new dialog types are inserted. The condition 'd > dialogFilter && d < dialogChangeAge' is the most robust expression.
findings.fix: When implementing, use 'd > dialogFilter && d < dialogChangeAge' as the range condition for the first check. This is equivalent to the named-constant list and survives insertion of new dialog types.

No other unexported functions are called cross-package.
task_01.all_unexported_functions_cross_package_a: CLEAN — only CheckIOAvailable() is called from outside internal/stat. readProcPidStat() and readProcPidIO() are called from buildProcPidResult() (Task 03) and Collector.Update() (Task 05), both within internal/stat.

task_05.CollectProcPidStat_value_6: CONFIRMED — iota sequence in the same const block: pgProcUptimeQuery (string const at iota=0), CollectNone=iota→1, CollectDiskstats→2, CollectNetdev→3, CollectFsstats→4, CollectLogtail→5. The task correctly notes this does NOT use continue, because the standard fallthrough c.Reset()+c.Update() already handles full view switches.

task_06.dialog_guard_current: CONFIRMED — current guard at dialog.go line 51: '(d > dialogFilter && d <= dialogChangeAge) && name != activity'. dialog constants: dialogFilter=2, dialogCancelQuery=3, dialogTerminateBackend=4, dialogCancelGroup=5, dialogTerminateGroup=6, dialogSetMask=7, dialogChangeAge=8. Restructuring as described is feasible.
task_06.keybindings_uppercase: CONFIRMED — keybindings.go lines 47-50 use 'B', 'N', 'F', 'L' as rune literals for uppercase bindings. 'S' follows the same pattern with no special modifier config.
